chunks.paper_title: Foreign Bank Entry in the Late Ottoman Empire: The Case of the Imperial Ottoman Bank
chunks.chunk_text: Abstract Before 1900, there were few foreign banks in the Ottoman Empire. The most important foreign bank was the Imperial Ottoman Bank. Many rival foreign banks established a presence over time, which could have undermined the power of the Imperial Ottoman Bank due to greater competition. This article examines how rival foreign banks affected the Imperial Ottoman Bank branches, using data on profits of these branches between 1895 and 1914.

chunks.paper_title: Foreign Bank Entry in the Late Ottoman Empire: The Case of the Imperial Ottoman Bank
chunks.chunk_text: n profits of these branches between 1895 and 1914. Empirical findings do not indicate that rival bank branches were related to lower profits for Imperial Ottoman Bank branches in the respective markets.

chunks.paper_title: COPPER EXPORT OF THE OTTOMAN EMPIRE TO ENGLAND VIA THE OTTOMAN BANK: ERGANI MINE 1880-1914
chunks.chunk_text: n bulk could meet the liquidity need more quickly. When the intermediaries to be used in exports were selected from the private sector, they applied high interest to the advance received by the state, reducing the profits to be obtained from copper exports. For this reason, it was decided to carry out exports together with the Ottoman Bank, which has branches abroad -especially in England-.

chunks.paper_title: COPPER EXPORT OF THE OTTOMAN EMPIRE TO ENGLAND VIA THE OTTOMAN BANK: ERGANI MINE 1880-1914
chunks.chunk_text: which has branches abroad -especially in England-. The export process carried out together with the Ottoman Bank seemed to be a suitable choice for the state as it had a capital structure that had no problem in meeting the liquidity need (advance) of the state as well as enabling the rapid sale of copper. In this study, the mine's export of copper, which is out of state needs, to abroad has been examined.

chunks.paper_title: COPPER EXPORT OF THE OTTOMAN EMPIRE TO ENGLAND VIA THE OTTOMAN BANK: ERGANI MINE 1880-1914
chunks.chunk_text: s out of state needs, to abroad has been examined. During the export process, 24 contracts were made between the Ottoman Bank and the Ottoman Empire between 1880 and 1914, and a total of 16,578 tons of copper was exported.

chunks.paper_title: A CITY RIGHT AT THE CORE OF GLOBAL, POLITICAL, ECONOMICAL AND SOCIAL CHANGES OF THE 19TH- CENTURY: ISTANBUL
chunks.chunk_text: design process for the earnings of foreign states. With this international commercial mobility of Galata, the new financial institutions were established there. The first bank of the Empire, Istanbul Bank (1849), was founded by Galata bankers in Istanbul. And then, The Ottoman Bank (1856), Ottoman Şahane Bank (1863), Ottoman General Corporation (1864), Ottoman General Credit Bank (1869), Thessaloniki Bank (1888) and Midilli Bankası (1891) were established in Istanbul.

chunks.paper_title: Cumhuriyet Gazetesine Göre Türkiye Cumhuriyeti’nin İlk Kâğıt Paralarının Basım Serüveni
chunks.chunk_text: When the Turkish Republic was founded the new state continued using Ottoman banknotes (evrak-ı nakdiye) for some time while preparing to print its own banknotes. In printing its own banknotes, the new state was motivated by Atatürk’s all-purpose motto of “catching up with the modern civilization level” in the realization of its goal. To this end, the Banknote Commission (Evrak-ı Nakdiye Komisyonu) was established under the direction of Mustafa Abdulhalik Bey.

chunks.paper_title: Osmanlı Devleti ve Fransa’da Sigortacılığın Gelişimi Hakkında Karşılaştırmalı Bir Analiz
chunks.chunk_text: , many new measures had to be taken for companies. In this period, we see that the arrangements made in France 1905 and 1906 were revised with many arrangements until the Second World War. This shows that France and the Ottoman Empire had the same difficulties in terms of controlling insurance companies. We hope that our study can contribute to understand capital mobility between the two countries and to understand the French influence in the Ottoman Banking and insurance market.

chunks.paper_title: Osmanlı Devleti ve Fransa’da Sigortacılığın Gelişimi Hakkında Karşılaştırmalı Bir Analiz
chunks.chunk_text: uence in the Ottoman Banking and insurance market. The fact that the French were in the leading position in the capital investments in the Ottoman Empire before the First World War, and that they showed a special interest in banking and insurance investments, makes researches about insurance companies important from now on. In addition, the UK's leadership position in the Ottoman insurance market led the French to gain more efficiency in the market.

chunks.paper_title: Kemunculan Perbankan Modern Dan Perannya Dalam Perekonomian Menjelang Keruntuhan Turki Utsmani (1847-1923)
chunks.chunk_text: Penelitian ini menjelaskan mengenai perkembangan bank modern di Turki Utsmani antara tahun 1847-1923. Penelitian ini menggunakan metode sejarah dan teknik penelitian studi kepustakaan. Hasil penelitian menunjukkan bahwa terdapat lima bank yang memiliki peran penting dalam perekonomian Turki Utsmani yakni Banque de Constantinople, Ottoman Bank, Banque Imperiale Ottomane (BIO), Bank Ziraat, dan Deutsche Bank.
